what_it_does: Acceptance test suite for the mode auto-detector. It exercises the file-discovered mode-detection rule registry, priority-based ordering, data-AST rule predicates, deterministic candidate detection from a synthetic activity signal, propose_mode integration with the off/suggest/auto toggle, fail-loud discovery validation, and drift-home documentation checks.
observations: The test is organized into eight numbered sections that mirror the claims in the module docstring.
It writes and deletes real rule files under mode_detection_rules/ to test discovery, removal, adversarial ordering, malformed rules, and unregistered modes.
A synthetic activity event is seeded with a timestamp offset from a fixed NOW to make idle_seconds deterministic.
It asserts that detect_mode_candidate is a pure read that emits no events and that propose_mode never emits resolve/approve/dispatch events.
It checks that mode_detection_rules/AGENTS.md and runtime/AGENTS.md mention each rule id and the detector functions.
It uses a global PASS counter incremented by a local check helper.
standouts: Adversarial filename test proving order is determined by declared priority, not listdir order.
Pure-read detector test: detect_mode_candidate emits/fires/switches nothing.
Suggest-not-switch semantics: under 'suggest' the candidate surfaces as a mode event without changing the live mode.
Fail-loud behavior for malformed rule files and for rules proposing unregistered modes.
conventions: Follows the project's acceptance-test narrative style with numbered claim sections.
Uses a local check(label, cond) helper with an assertion and a printed ok line.
Uses tempfile for store isolation and try/finally blocks to clean up dropped rule files.
Imports runtime components and the FsStore directly rather than mocking them.
concerns: Tests mutate the real mode_detection_rules/ directory, which could interfere with parallel test runs or leave files if a crash occurs inside the try block.
open().write() is used without a context manager in several places.
The acceptance checks depend on the presence and exact ids/priorities of seed rules background/focus/listening.
AGENTS.md drift checks are brittle because they rely on specific backtick-wrapped strings in documentation files.
The global NOW and PASS variables make the module non-reentrant.
notes: A runnable specification that the mode auto-detector is a file-discovered, priority-ordered, data-AST registry that only proposes candidates to the existing toggle.
questions: What is the exact contract of ModeDetectionRuleRegistry.discover and rediscover?
How does act.detect_mode_candidate compute the signal fields idle_seconds and inbox?
What does Suite.set_rhm_config do beyond storing a config value?
Which event kinds are considered mode-lifecycle versus consequential verbs?
How are AGENTS.md files expected to be kept in sync automatically?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it verifies each numbered claim about the detector, registry, toggle integration, and drift homes.
apparent_themes: File-discovered registries as the source of truth
A single data-AST predicate language for rules
Deterministic pure reads over synthetic signals
Fail-loud validation instead of silent skipping
Separation between detection/proposal and consequential action
Drift-home documentation as a first-class acceptance concern
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish that mode detection is a clean, declarative, file-discovered subsystem whose only job is to read activity signals and feed the existing off/suggest/auto toggle, never to switch modes or trigger downstream actions on its own.
novelty: It treats detection rules as a file-discovered registry ordered by explicit priority and validated against a shared data-AST grammar, and it includes adversarial-filename and documentation-drift acceptance checks alongside behavioral tests.
contribution: A concrete acceptance specification that a fused system can run to confirm the mode detection registry is discovered from files, ordered by priority, uses validated data-AST conditions, produces deterministic candidates, respects the off/suggest/auto toggle without direct mode setting, fails loudly on malformed rules and unregistered modes, and keeps its drift-home docs in sync.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test file for the mode auto-detector covering registry discovery, priority ordering, data-AST rule validation, deterministic candidate detection, toggle integration, fail-loud behavior, and documentation drift.
intention_for_embedding: To verify that the mode auto-detector behaves as a file-discovered, order-bearing, data-AST registry that proposes candidates to the existing toggle without emitting consequential actions.

symbols.does: Local assertion helper that increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: Evaluates a boolean condition, asserts with a labeled failure message, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints an ok line for each passing check.

symbols.does: Creates a new Suite with a temporary FsStore and discovered nodes.
symbols.description: Initializes a fresh FsStore in a temporary directory, discovers nodes from the nodes directory, and returns a new Suite instance so each test section starts from a clean state.

symbols.does: Appends a synthetic chat event at a deterministic past timestamp.
symbols.description: Writes a single chat event to the store with a timestamp set to NOW minus age_s seconds, ensuring the detector sees a controlled idle duration regardless of config-emit ordering.

symbols.does: Returns a Suite preset to a mode and autodetect toggle with seeded activity.
symbols.description: Creates a fresh suite, sets the current mode and MODE_AUTODETECT toggle, seeds a long-idle activity event by default, and returns the suite for propose_mode integration tests.
